description_long: This water cooler is a freestanding, rectangular appliance with a robust, industrial design, likely intended for high-traffic urban environments. The front features a large, dark-tinted glass or plastic door with a visible internal fan and a metallic dispensing area below, as well as a compact control panel with several buttons and a digital display. The sides are plain, with minimal detailing except for a few access panels and a handle, while the rear has ventilation grilles for airflow. The unit stands on four short, sturdy legs, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall color is a uniform light gray or off-white, giving it a clean, utilitarian appearance. No water bottle is visible on top, suggesting it may be a plumbed-in or bottleless model.
description: A robust, rectangular water cooler with a glass front door, control panel, and metallic dispensing area, finished in light gray. Designed for urban or industrial settings, it features ventilation grilles and sturdy legs.
description_view_0: The left side view shows a plain, flat panel with a handle and a small circular access port, with minimal detailing.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the large glass or plastic door, internal fan, metallic dispensing area, and a compact control panel with buttons and a display.
description_view_2: The right side view displays another flat panel with a handle and a small access port, as well as a recessed area possibly for maintenance.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals two ventilation grilles and a plain, solid panel, with no visible controls or dispensing features.
attribute_dispenser_spout_type: rectangular metallic dispensing area with a possible push-button or lever mechanism
attribute_control_panel_design: Compact rectangular panel with several small buttons and a digital display, located on the upper right of the front face.
attribute_brand_logo: A circular logo is visible above the door, but the brand name is not clearly readable.
